name: Mailto

values.name: bcc
values.comment:
Adds blind carbon copies to the mailto

    mailto "partner@test.mail"
        |> bcc [ "bcc@test.mail", "secretfriend@test.mail" ]
        |> toString
    -- "mailto:partner@test.mail?bcc=bcc@test.mail,secretfriend@test.mail"

values.name: body
values.comment:
Adds a body to the mailto

    mailto "partner@test.mail"
        |> body "It will be a spicy nam dtok muu salad."
        |> toString
    -- "mailto:partner@test.mail?body=It will be a spicy nam dtok muu salad."

values.name: cc
values.comment:
Adds carbon copies to the mailto

    mailto "partner@test.mail"
        |> cc [ "cc@test.mail", "mutualfried@test.mail" ]
        |> toString
    -- "mailto:partner@test.mail?cc=cc@test.mail,mutualfried@test.mail"

values.name: mailto
values.comment: Constructs an empty Mailto with no parameters. It's the `singleton` of `Mailto`.

values.name: mailtoMultiple
values.comment: Constructs an empty Mailto with no parameters, but with mailtoMultiple recipients. It's the `singleton` of `Mailto`.

values.name: subject
values.comment:
Adds a subject to the mailto

    mailto "partner@test.mail"
        |> subject "I want to cook you dinner"
        |> toString
    -- "mailto:partner@test.mail?subject=I want to cook you dinner"

values.name: toHref
values.comment: For convenience, you can turn a `Mailto` into an `Html.Attribute` as well

values.name: toString
values.comment: After composing a `Mailto`, consume a string
